name: Asma Jilani v. Government of the Punjab
era: Transition — From Martial Law to the 1973 Constitution (1969–1973)
key_provisions: Yahya's regime branded usurpation; its acts subject to condonation, not validation
Reasserted that sovereignty belongs to the people under law
demise: Its principles were strained by later validations (Nusrat Bhutto 1977, Zafar Ali Shah 2000) but never formally abandoned.
